inputs.label: Labor Rate (per hour)
inputs.businessContext: Hourly labor charge rate to customers. Based on industry average for auto repair shops (e.g., 2024 rates $80-$150).

inputs.label: Labor Hours Billed (per month)
inputs.businessContext: Total billed labor hours per month. Typical shop with 2-5 technicians bills 300-800 hours.

inputs.label: Parts Revenue (per month)
inputs.businessContext: Monthly revenue from parts sales. Parts markup typically 30-50% over cost.

inputs.label: Parts Cost (per month)
inputs.businessContext: Monthly cost of parts sold. Includes wholesale purchase cost.

inputs.label: Labor Cost per Hour (technician wage + burden)
inputs.businessContext: Effective hourly cost of technician including wages, taxes, benefits. Typically 25-40% of labor rate.

inputs.label: Monthly Overhead (rent, utilities, insurance, etc.)
inputs.businessContext: Fixed monthly operating expenses excluding labor and parts cost.

inputs.label: Warranty Returns / Rework (% of revenue)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of revenue lost to warranty repairs or rework. Industry benchmark <3%.

inputs.label: Inventory Shrinkage (% of parts cost)
inputs.businessContext: Parts loss due to theft, damage, or misplacement. Typical 0.5-2%.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: User's confidence in input data accuracy. Adjusts final margin for uncertainty.

thresholds.grossMarginPercent < 40: Low gross margin warning: check pricing and cost structure.
thresholds.laborMarginPercent < 50: Labor margin below 50%: labor rate may be too low or efficiency poor.
thresholds.partsMarkupPercent < 30: Parts markup below 30%: consider increasing parts prices.
thresholds.warrantyReturnsPercent > 5: High warranty returns: investigate quality issues.
thresholds.inventoryShrinkagePercent > 2: High inventory shrinkage: improve inventory controls.

suggestedActions: If labor margin < 50%: increase labor rate or improve technician efficiency.
If parts markup < 30%: renegotiate supplier pricing or increase retail markup.
If warranty returns > 3%: implement quality control process.
If inventory shrinkage > 1.5%: conduct physical inventory and improve security.
